variations.prompt: Use Image 2 as the identity and pose reference of the person, and Image 1 as the scene reference.CORE SYSTEM RULE: Analyze Image 2.- IF IMAGE 2 CONTAINS A PERSON/HUMAN:  Create a photorealistic image of the person from Image 2 integrated inside the yellow-themed bedroom display shown in Image 1.  Preserve the exact environment of Image 1, including the yellow walls, bed frame, yellow cartoon pillows, grey blanket, zig-zag patterned bedsheet, floor, lighting, perspective, framing, camera angle, and overall composition.  The number of people in the generated image must exactly match the number of reference photos provided in Image 2.  CRITICAL REQUIREMENT: The face of the generated person in the final output MUST look exactly like a photograph of the person in Image 2. You must preserve the exact facial features, face shape, eye color, nose shape, lip shape, skin tone, hairstyle, and unique identity from Image 2 without any alterations or modifications. The subject's face must be a 100% perfect match to Image 2.  Preserve the original clothing, outfit, accessories, colors, and textures from Image 2 for the visible upper body. If Image 2 is a half-body or close-up photo where the lower body (pants, legs, shoes) is not visible, complete the outfit by generating matching long pants (trousers) and closed formal shoes (like sneakers or dress shoes) that harmonize with the style of the upper body outfit to ensure a polite and neat appearance. Do not generate bare legs, shorts, sandals, or bare feet.  Replicate the exact pose, body orientation, leg placement, hand placement, head tilt, and physical gestures of the person from Image 2. Place the person naturally in front of the bed in the environment of Image 1, matching the perspective, lighting, and scale.  IMPORTANT RULES:  - Replicate the exact pose, hand gestures, and body orientation of the person from Image 2.  - Place the person naturally in front of the bed inside the scene.  - Change only the identity and environment while matching the subject's pose from Image 2.- IF IMAGE 2 DOES NOT CONTAIN A PERSON/HUMAN (e.g., it is an inanimate object like a phone, pen, glass, bottle, cup, book, etc.):  DO NOT generate any human or person in the final output image. The final image must have ZERO people. No faces, no bodies, no human hands.  Remove the person from Image 1 entirely.  Take the main object shown in Image 2 and place it naturally inside the yellow-themed bedroom scene of Image 1 (for example, place the phone or pen lying on the yellow bed or on the floor). Match the perspective, scale, lighting, shadows, and depth of field of the environment.The output must be a professional exhibition photograph with realistic lighting, soft natural shadows, sharp focus on the subjects, and high-resolution details. Vertical portrait orientation, 9:16 aspect ratio.

variations.name: Studio
variations.prompt: Use Image 2 as the identity and pose reference of the person, and Image 1 as the scene, composition, and camera angle reference.CORE SYSTEM RULE: Analyze Image 2.- IF IMAGE 2 CONTAINS A PERSON/HUMAN:  Create a highly realistic image of the person from Image 2 integrated inside the red retro music room shown in Image 1.  Preserve the exact composition, camera angle, perspective, framing, subject scale, and spatial placement from Image 1.  The scene is a small enclosed retro music room with bright red walls, decorated with vinyl records, vintage posters, music-themed artwork, metal handrails, and a disco ball mounted on the ceiling.  The camera is positioned directly above the subject, looking straight downward from the ceiling using an ultra-wide fisheye lens. Maintain the same dramatic top-down perspective distortion, lens compression, room geometry, and visual depth as Image 1.  Keep all wall decorations, vinyl record placements, posters, handrails, disco ball, lighting, shadows, reflections, room proportions, and environmental details exactly as shown in Image 1.  CRITICAL REQUIREMENT (FACE MATCH): The face of the generated person in the final output MUST look exactly like a photograph of the person in Image 2. You must preserve the exact facial features, face shape, eye color, nose shape, lip shape, skin tone, hairstyle, and unique identity from Image 2 without any alterations or modifications. The subject's face must be a 100% perfect match to Image 2.  Preserve the original clothing, outfit, accessories, colors, and textures from Image 2 for the visible upper body. If Image 2 is a half-body or close-up photo where the lower body (pants, legs, shoes) is not visible, complete the outfit by generating matching long pants (trousers) and closed formal shoes (like sneakers or dress shoes) that harmonize with the style of the upper body outfit to ensure a polite and neat appearance. Do not generate bare legs, shorts, sandals, or bare feet.    FRAMING & ANATOMY (UPRIGHT TOP-DOWN VIEW): Replicate the pose, body orientation, and physical gestures of the person from Image 2. The person must stand on the floor of the room, looking straight up at the ceiling camera. To maintain correct human anatomy, the person's head must be positioned near the top-center of the frame, and their neck, shoulders, chest, and legs must extend downwards from their head, with their feet standing on the floor. The face must be oriented right-side up (forehead at the top, chin at the bottom) looking directly up at the camera. The neck and shoulders must extend downwards from the chin to prevent any upside-down or twisted body distortions.    IMPORTANT RULES:  - Replicate the pose, gestures, and body orientation of the person from Image 2.  - Place the person's head near the top-center of the frame, with the body extending downwards (upright top-down view).  - Maintain the top-down fisheye camera perspective from Image 1.  - Change only the identity and environment while matching the subject's pose from Image 2.- IF IMAGE 2 DOES NOT CONTAIN A PERSON/HUMAN (e.g., it is an inanimate object like a phone, pen, glass, bottle, cup, book, etc.):  DO NOT generate any human or person in the final output image. The final image must have ZERO people. No faces, no bodies, no human hands.  Remove the person from Image 1 entirely.  Take the main object shown in Image 2 and place it naturally inside the red retro music room of Image 1 (for example, place the phone or pen lying on the floor, handrail, or speaker). Match the top-down fisheye perspective, scale, lighting, shadows, and depth of field of the environment.The output must be a high-resolution professional photograph with realistic lighting, shadows, reflections, natural depth of field, and sharp focus on the subject. Vertical portrait orientation, 9:16 aspect ratio.

variations.name: Japan Road Trip
variations.prompt: Use Image 2 as the identity and pose reference of the person, and Image 1 as the scene reference.CORE SYSTEM RULE: Analyze Image 2.- IF IMAGE 2 CONTAINS A PERSON/HUMAN:  Create a highly photorealistic close-up half-body selfie image of the person from Image 2 integrated inside the scenic Japanese mountain road shown in Image 1.  The environment is a winding mountain road in Japan during spring with pink cherry blossom (sakura) trees in full bloom framing the scene.  In the background, Mt. Fuji with a majestic snow-capped peak is visible under a warm glowing sunset sky.  The road is wet and highly reflective. Two modern sports cars (a white sports car and a blue sports car) are captured driving on the winding road.  To the left, a traditional red Japanese pagoda and a red torii gate stand elegantly among the pink sakura trees.    CRITICAL VISUAL EFFECT (FOREGROUND PETALS): Only a light sprinkling of a few subtle pink cherry blossom (sakura) petals should float in the foreground, with one or two petals gently overlapping the person's clothing or hair. Keep the petals sparse and delicate so they do not clutter the image or block the subject.    LIGHTING & BLENDING: The lighting and shadows on the person must blend seamlessly with the environment. Apply soft warm sunset highlights (golden-hour orange, soft pink, and warm gold highlights) reflecting on the person's skin, face, hair, and clothing, matching the natural light coming from the sunset behind Mt. Fuji. The subject must not look cut-out or pasted; they must blend into the atmospheric environment perfectly.    CRITICAL REQUIREMENT (FACE MATCH): The face of the generated person in the final output MUST look exactly like a photograph of the person in Image 2. You must preserve the exact facial features, face shape, eye color, nose shape, lip shape, skin tone, hairstyle, and unique identity from Image 2 without any alterations or modifications. The subject's face must be a 100% perfect match to Image 2.    FRAMING & COMPOSITION (HALF-BODY SELFIE): The output image must be a moderate half-body selfie shot (from the chest up) placing the person in the bottom-left corner of the frame (occupying roughly 35% of the bottom-left area). The person should look moderately sized so they do not overpower the image. This ensures the background elements (Mt. Fuji, the winding road, the sports cars, and the pagoda) are clearly visible and not blocked. The lower body (legs, pants, shoes) must NOT be visible.    Preserve the original clothing, outfit, accessories, colors, and textures from Image 2 for the visible upper body (jacket/shirt).    Replicate the exact pose, body orientation, hand placement (holding up a peace sign if they do so in Image 2), head tilt, and physical gestures of the person from Image 2. The subject is positioned in the close-up foreground as if taking a selfie in front of the gorgeous Japanese landscape.  IMPORTANT RULES:  - Replicate the pose, gestures, and body orientation of the person from Image 2.  - Position the person in the bottom-left corner of the frame at a moderate size, as if taking a selfie (half-body chest-up shot).  - Change only the identity and environment while matching the subject's pose from Image 2.- IF IMAGE 2 DOES NOT CONTAIN A PERSON/HUMAN (e.g., it is an inanimate object like a phone, pen, glass, bottle, cup, book, etc.):  DO NOT generate any human or person in the final output image. The final image must have ZERO people. No faces, no bodies, no human hands.  Remove the person from Image 1 entirely.  Take the main object shown in Image 2 and place it naturally on the wet road or beside the road in Image 1. Match the perspective, scale, lighting, shadows, and depth of field of the environment.The output must be a professional high-quality photograph with realistic lighting, soft shadows, sharp focus on the foreground subject, and high-resolution details. Vertical portrait orientation, 9:16 aspect ratio.
